title: Asteranthemum dahuricum
taxonomy: Asteranthemum dahuricum belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family, which consists of flowering plants. This family is one of the largest and most widespread in the plant kingdom, comprising around 23,000 species. Asteranthemum dahuricum is also classified as a member of the genus Asteranthemum, which is a small genus of flowering plants. The exact classification within the genus may be more specific or require further research. Further classification may include the species' order, class, and kingdom, but this information is not readily available.
uses: Asteranthemum dahuricum has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Asia, for its potential health benefits. Its leaves and stems are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. intro: Asteranthemum dahuricum, also known as Dahurian Asteranthemum or Dahurian Asteranthe, is a perennial plant belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities that may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis, wound healing, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Asteranthemum dahuricum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties.
